meta.title: Original Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance Practice Question Bank
meta.chapter: NCERT Class 12 Physics Chapter 2 - Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance
meta.licenseNote: Original generated educational questions. No copied coaching or previous-year content.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Electrostatic Potential?
questions.answer: V = W/q0
questions.solution: Electrostatic Potential uses V = W/q0. Potential is work per unit charge.
questions.commonTrap: Do not confuse potential with potential energy.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Potential Difference?
questions.answer: Delta V = W/q
questions.solution: Potential Difference uses Delta V = W/q. Potential difference measures energy change per unit charge.
questions.commonTrap: Sign depends on direction.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Point Charge Potential?
questions.answer: V = kQ/r
questions.solution: Point Charge Potential uses V = kQ/r. Potential falls as 1/r and keeps charge sign.
questions.commonTrap: Use algebraic Q, not magnitude only.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Field-Potential Relation?
questions.answer: E = -dV/dr
questions.solution: Field-Potential Relation uses E = -dV/dr. Field points toward decreasing potential.
questions.commonTrap: The negative sign matters.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Potential Energy of Two Charges?
questions.answer: U = kq1q2/r
questions.solution: Potential Energy of Two Charges uses U = kq1q2/r. Energy stored in assembling two charges.
questions.commonTrap: Count signs carefully.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with System Potential Energy?
questions.answer: U = sum k qi qj/rij
questions.solution: System Potential Energy uses U = sum k qi qj/rij. Add energy for every distinct pair.
questions.commonTrap: Do not double-count pairs.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Dipole Energy?
questions.answer: U = -pE cos theta
questions.solution: Dipole Energy uses U = -pE cos theta. Dipole energy is minimum when p aligns with E.
questions.commonTrap: Theta is between p and E.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Capacitance?
questions.answer: C = Q/V
questions.solution: Capacitance uses C = Q/V. Capacitance measures charge stored per volt.
questions.commonTrap: Capacitance is not caused by Q alone.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Spherical Conductor Capacitance?
questions.answer: C = 4 pi epsilon0 R
questions.solution: Spherical Conductor Capacitance uses C = 4 pi epsilon0 R. Larger isolated sphere has larger capacitance.
questions.commonTrap: This is for isolated sphere.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Parallel Plate Capacitance?
questions.answer: C = epsilon0 A/d
questions.solution: Parallel Plate Capacitance uses C = epsilon0 A/d. Bigger area stores more charge; bigger gap stores less.
questions.commonTrap: Neglect edge effects.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Dielectric Capacitor?
questions.answer: C = K epsilon0 A/d
questions.solution: Dielectric Capacitor uses C = K epsilon0 A/d. Dielectric increases capacitance by factor K.
questions.commonTrap: K is not capacitance.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Series Capacitors?
questions.solution: Series Capacitors uses 1/Ceq = 1/C1 + 1/C2 + .... Same charge flows on series capacitors.
questions.commonTrap: Do not add C directly in series.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Parallel Capacitors?
questions.solution: Parallel Capacitors uses Ceq = C1 + C2 + .... Parallel capacitors share same voltage.
questions.commonTrap: Do not use reciprocal for parallel.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Capacitor Energy?
questions.solution: Capacitor Energy uses U = 1/2 CV^2 = Q^2/(2C) = 1/2 QV. Energy is stored in electric field.
questions.commonTrap: Pick formula based on constant Q or V.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.

questions.question: Which formula is most directly linked with Electric Field Energy Density?
questions.answer: u = 1/2 epsilon0 E^2
questions.solution: Electric Field Energy Density uses u = 1/2 epsilon0 E^2. Energy is distributed through the field volume.
questions.commonTrap: Use dielectric permittivity when dielectric fills space.
questions.shortcut: Choose the model, convert units and check the graph/constraint.
